name: Vietnam
overview: Vietnam moved rapidly from Decree 13/2023 (its first consolidated personal data protection decree) to a full Law on Personal Data Protection passed in June 2025 and effective 1 January 2026. The regime is consent-heavy, restricts data trading outright, and layers onto the Cybersecurity Law's localisation requirements. The Ministry of Public Security (A05) is the enforcement authority.

regulator.name: Ministry of Public Security — Department of Cybersecurity (A05)

legal_instruments.title: Law on Personal Data Protection (2025)
legal_instruments.summary: Vietnam's first personal data protection law (passed 26 June 2025, effective 1 Jan 2026): consent-centric processing, prohibition on buying/selling personal data, impact assessment dossiers, cross-border transfer assessments, and fines up to 5% of prior-year revenue for certain violations such as unlawful data trading.

legal_instruments.title: Decree No. 13/2023/ND-CP on Personal Data Protection
legal_instruments.summary: First comprehensive PDP instrument: basic and sensitive data categories, consent requirements, data subject rights, DPIA and cross-border transfer impact assessment dossiers filed with A05. Remains the operative detail pending the 2025 Law's implementing decrees.

legal_instruments.title: Law on Cybersecurity (2018) and Decree 53/2022/ND-CP
legal_instruments.summary: Requires certain domestic and foreign service providers to store specified user data in Vietnam and, for foreign providers meeting conditions, to establish a local presence.

developments.title: Law on Personal Data Protection takes effect
developments.summary: Vietnam's PDP Law came into force, elevating Decree 13's framework to statute with revenue-based fines and an explicit ban on personal data trading.

developments.title: National Assembly passes the PDP Law
developments.summary: The National Assembly approved the Law on Personal Data Protection with near-unanimous support, setting a 1 January 2026 effective date.

penalties: Under the 2025 Law: fines up to 5% of previous year's revenue for violations such as unlawful cross-border transfer or data trading; administrative sanctions detail to follow in implementing decrees.
cross_border: Cross-border transfer of Vietnamese citizens' data requires a transfer impact assessment dossier filed with the Ministry of Public Security, subject to inspection; localisation obligations under the Cybersecurity Law apply to specified services.
